steps.title: Set y = 0 to find the x-intercept
steps.content:
The x-intercept occurs where the line crosses the x-axis, which is when $y = 0$. Substitute $y = 0$ into the equation:
$$\frac{4}{5}x + \frac{1}{3}(0) = 1$$
$$\frac{4}{5}x = 1$$

steps.title: Solve for x
steps.content:
Multiply both sides by $\frac{5}{4}$ (the reciprocal of $\frac{4}{5}$):
$$x = 1 \cdot \frac{5}{4} = \frac{5}{4}$$
